name: El Comptat and the Embalse de Beniarrés - Circular from Muro de Alcoy
name_original: El Comptat y el Embalse de Beniarrés — Circular desde Muro de Alcoy
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Muro De Alcoy are situated in a diverse landscape at the foot of the Serra de Mariola, featuring mountainous terrain, verdant valleys, and historic towns. The region offers a network of mostly paved roads with varied elevation profiles, from gentle ascents to demanding climbs. Cyclists can explore natural parks, river valleys, and the El Comtat region, which is known for its favorable climate and mountainous topography. Best road cycling routes around Muro De Alcoy The most popular road cycling route is El Comptat and the Embalse de Beniarrés - Circular from Muro de Alcoy, a 31.9 miles (51.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 42 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the El Comtat region and passes by the Embalse de Beniarrés. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Guadalest Castle – Sa Creueta Pass (780 m) loop from Cocentaina, a difficult 63.9 miles (103.0 km) path. This route includes significant elevation gain, traversing challenging passes and offering views of the surrounding mountains. Local road cyclists also love the Pego–Sagra Road – Gallinera Valley loop from Cocentaina, a 67.3 miles (108.3 km) trail leading through the Gallinera Valley, often completed in about 5 hours 33 minutes. Road cycling around Muro De Alcoy is defined by mountainous terrain, verdant valleys, and historic towns. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to difficult climbs. The routes in Muro De Alcoy are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 7,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Muro De Alcoy's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Muro de Alcoy?
A: Muro de Alcoy offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with over 140 options in the immediate vicinity. These routes cater to various skill levels, from moderate to difficult.

Q: What kind of terrain and elevation can I expect on road cycling routes in Muro de Alcoy?
A: The region features a diverse landscape with mostly paved roads. You'll encounter mountainous terrain and verdant valleys, offering varied elevation profiles. Routes can range from gentle ascents to demanding climbs, with some challenging sections for experienced riders.

Q: Are there challenging climbs for experienced road cyclists?
A: Yes, Muro de Alcoy is known for its demanding ascents. The region is favored by professional cycling teams for winter training, and you can find climbs like the famous Xorret de Catí, which has gradients of up to 24% and is regularly featured in the Vuelta a España.

Q: Are there easier road cycling routes suitable for beginners or casual riders?
A: While the region is known for its challenging terrain, there are options for less experienced riders. Approximately 10 of the routes are classified as easy, and 65 are moderate, offering more manageable distances and elevation gains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e891677547/el-comptat-and-the-embalse-de-beniarres-circular-from-muro-de-alcoy" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Comptat and the Embalse de Beniarrés - Circular from Muro de Alcoy&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate route.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while cycling in Muro de Alcoy?
A: Cyclists are rewarded with stunning views of the Serra de Mariola Natural Park, Cocentaina Castle, and the Benicadell peak. Routes often pass through picturesque historic towns like Bocairent and Planes. You might also spot vultures in the Sierra Mariola area. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/210458" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beniarrés Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is another notable feature along some routes.

Q: Are there any historical sites or attractions near the road cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in history. You can explore the ancient snow storage facility of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2546615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cava Gran de Agres&lt;/a&gt;, the intriguing artificial &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4611508" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova de les Finestres&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4053198" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Lorcha Railway Station&lt;/a&gt;, which is now part of the Serpis Greenway.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Muro de Alcoy?
A: The road cycling routes around Muro de Alcoy are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 100 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscape, the mix of challenging climbs and scenic valleys, and the opportunity to explore natural parks and historic towns.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in Muro de Alcoy?
A: Muro de Alcoy benefits from a generally smooth climate, making it suitable for outdoor pursuits for much of the year. The favorable climate and mountainous topography make it a popular destination for cycling, especially during the cooler months for training.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e891677547/el-comptat-and-the-embalse-de-beniarres-circular-from-muro-de-alcoy" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Comptat and the Embalse de Beniarrés - Circular from Muro de Alcoy&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option. The Sierra Mariola Natural Park also features routes like the 'Sierra Mariola Circle', a challenging 90km ride.

Q: Can I bring my dog on road cycling routes in Muro de Alcoy?
A: Road cycling routes are primarily designed for cyclists on paved roads, which may not be ideal or safe for dogs, especially given the varied terrain and potential traffic. For activities with dogs, you might consider exploring the numerous hiking trails within the Sierra de Mariola Natural Park.

Q: Where can I park my car when cycling in Muro de Alcoy?
A: While specific parking locations for cyclists are not detailed, Muro de Alcoy is a town with various public parking options. It's advisable to check local parking regulations upon arrival or consult maps for designated parking areas near your chosen route's starting point.

Q: Is Muro de Alcoy accessible by public transport for cyclists?
A: Muro de Alcoy has public transport connections, but specific information regarding bicycle transport on these services is not readily available. It's recommended to check with local bus or train operators for their policies on carrying bicycles if you plan to use public transport to reach the area.
tips: Very picturesque and demanding stretch. With a good surface, riding here is very entertaining, but you will have to overcome slopes over 20%, both up and down, so make sure your brakes are in optimal condition.
